# VER-QUIC-INT-0005 - QUIC Interop Retry Child-Process Verification

## Scope

Verify the managed child-process `retry` testcase, the one-valid-Retry emission gate, the client replay, the replayed Initial acceptance, and exit-code honesty only after post-Retry handshake completion.

## Requirements Verified

- REQ-QUIC-INT-0012

## Verification Method

Focused requirement-home tests, build execution, and artifact inspection.

## Preconditions

- REQ-QUIC-INT-0007, REQ-QUIC-INT-0008, REQ-QUIC-INT-0010, REQ-QUIC-INT-0011, REQ-QUIC-API-0002, REQ-QUIC-API-0004, REQ-QUIC-CRT-0121, and REQ-QUIC-CRT-0122 remain green.
- The harness can dispatch `retry` through the managed child-process path.
- Loopback UDP sockets and the test TLS material are available.

## Procedure or Approach

- Run the new retry requirement-home test and confirm the server issues one Retry while the client observes exactly one Retry transition and the handshake completes on the replayed path.
- Run the current interop and CRT guard tests that protect the supported floor.
- Run the full `REQ_QUIC_CRT_` sweep.
- Render and validate the SpecTrace JSON and Markdown artifacts after any canonical JSON updates.

## Expected Result

The child-process harness proves exactly one valid Retry on the real managed path, the client replays exactly once, the server accepts the replayed Initial, and exit code 0 is returned only after post-Retry handshake completion. Unsupported testcases continue to return `127`.
